none.justification: Matches all required elements: SYSCALL_DEFINE3(read) at fs/read_write.c:621, ksys_read at :602, vfs_read at :450, syscall_64.tbl line 11 mapping, and in-tree macro-wrapper evidence. It includes extra dispatch/macro-generated context, but it correctly marks those as generated/dispatch context and does not assert the v6.9-only x64_sys_call path.

ccodegraph.justification: Gives the exact required 3-hop chain with correct file:line references and the non-generated table row arch/x86/entry/syscalls/syscall_64.tbl:11. It also states the macro mechanism linking SYSCALL_DEFINE3 to sys_read/__x64_sys_read, which satisfies the rubric.

codegraph.justification: Contains the required chain and line numbers, the correct syscall_64.tbl mapping, and macro-wrapper evidence. Although it frames the answer as hardware entry through vfs_read and includes generated wrapper symbols, those are identified as macro-generated context rather than replacing the required SYSCALL_DEFINE3 -> ksys_read -> vfs_read path.

cbm.justification: Correctly identifies SYSCALL_DEFINE3(read) at fs/read_write.c:621, ksys_read at :602 with call site :623, and the syscall_64.tbl:11 mapping. It fails to give the vfs_read definition line fs/read_write.c:450, listing only the call site :613, and its macro mechanism is asserted without the in-tree syscalls.h/syscall_wrapper.h evidence called out by the rubric.
